observations.recommendation: 16. Make the necessary actions to request for write-off of the dormant Due from NGAs account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the perpetual inventory method in the recording of regular purchases of supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and to promote better control and monitoring in the receipt and issuance of inventories.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
a. 	Inventory Committee to follow the general guidelines and procedures on inventory taking, recognition of assets found at station, disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items without available record of accountability, and reconciliation of records/reports for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. 	Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal GSO to prepare and update the PPELCs and PCs, respectively, for each PPE account, and to reconcile their records regularly or every quarter, if possible.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the required supporting documents for the three DVs and for future disbursements, follow strictly the provisions of RA No. 12009.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to expedite the tracing of MOAs covering the unutilized fund transfers presented in Annex B, and/or coordinate with the source agencies to determine its proper disposition.  Otherwise, revert the unutilized balances to the source agencies in accordance with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. 	Immediately deposit the remaining cash balance on hand as of April 30, 2025 in the total amount of ₱1.470 million with the depository bank and furnish the Audit Team with copy of the deposit slips; and
b. 	Henceforth, deposit all collections intact and not later than the following banking day with the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Paragraphs 1 and 4 of Section 69 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile records on the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the Pagibig.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the concerned accountable officers to settle their obligation immediately, otherwise, the amount due will be deducted from whatever claims they have against the Municipality.  Henceforth, require all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances in accordance with the provisions of stated above.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile and account for the discrepancy of the Cash Local Treasury account between their records amounting to ₱110,426.83 and thereafter record the necessary adjustments, if warranted.  Henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of both records to establish the correctness of the Cash Local Treasury account.
We recommended and Management agreed to continue to exert effort in the prompt settlement of NS, ND and NC as the need arises.
1. Issue ICS on tangible property and ARE for PPE for existing property and future purchases.
2. Reconcile records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the BIR.

3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record/adjust the stale checks in the books amounting to ₱272,140.29 and monitor recording of cancellation to avoid inadvertent payments or replacement of stale checks.
4. Submit the duly received distribution lists required for the disbursement vouchers and attach duly received distribution lists for future identical disbursements.

5. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to Immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for properly analyzed and verified reconciling items noted during the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS); and

6. Exert more effort in analyzing the nature and details of the unidentified book reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

7. Require the POs to be duly signed by the signatories for subsequent transactions and submit the following:

8. Additional supporting documents to justify the difference between number of pax paid and the number of personnel ordered to attend the activity;

9. Menu for the breakfast, lunch and dinner was not attached; and

10. Attendance Sheet.

Submit the following required supporting documents for the repair various equipment and motor vehicles totaling P157,108.78:

11. Pre-repair inspection reports;

12. Waste Material reports; and

13. Post-inspection reports.
14. Reconcile records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the GSIS.

15. Cause the settlement of the ND Nos. 2024-001 to 006-101(2023) issued for the excess travelling expenses amounting to P10,800.00 and strictly comply with the provisions of EO No. 77 on subsequent travel claims.

17. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic, preferably quarterly, reconciliation of the budget and accounting records; and

18. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer submit the SAAOB to the Municipal Accountant at an earlier date that will allow the Municipal Accountant to prepare the financial reports and submit the same on time pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2010-001.
Make the necessary actions to request for write-off of the dormant Due from NGAs account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic, preferably quarterly, reconciliation of the budget and accounting records; and

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer submit the SAAOB to the Municipal Accountant at an earlier date that will allow the Municipal Accountant to prepare the financial reports and submit the same on time pursuant to COA Accounting Circular Letter No. 2010-001.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant strictly follow the perpetual inventory method in the recording of regular purchases of supplies and materials to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of inventories;
Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer (GSO) prepare/maintain SLCs and SCs, respectively, for each type of inventory item, and conduct reconciliation of their records to determine whether the actual assets agree with the recorded accountability; and
Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories and prepare and submit the RPCI for each inventory account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Reorganize the Inventory Committee purposely to conduct complete physical count of assets and to avail one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 and submit the corresponding inventory report to COA; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal GSO to reconcile their both records and do it regularly or every quarter, if possible.
We recommended and the management agreed to issue ICS on tangible property and ARE for PPE for existing property and future purchases.
We recommended and the management agreed to reconcile records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the BIR.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer  deposit all collections daily and intact with the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Paragraphs 1 and 4 of Section 69 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer:
Reconcile and account for the discrepancy of the Cash Local Treasury account between the accounting record and the cashbook amounting to ₱0.672 million and thereafter record the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
Henceforth, conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to establish the correctness of both records.
We recommended and the management agreed to reconcile records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the Pagibig.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the concerned accountable officers to settle their obligation immediately, otherwise, deduct from whatever amount due them their payable to the government.  Henceforth, require all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances in accordance with the provisions of EO No. 77, COA Circular No. 97-002 and PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant record/adjust the stale checks in the books amounting to ₱272,140.29 and monitor recording of cancellation to avoid inadvertent payments or replacement of stale checks.

We recommended and the management agreed to submit the duly received distribution lists required for the disbursement vouchers and attach duly received distribution lists for future identical disbursements.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant will:
Immediately prepare correcting/adjusting entries for properly analyzed and verified reconciling items noted during the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS); and
Exert more effort in analysing the nature and details of the unidentified book reconciling items and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the POs to be duly signed by the signatories for subsequent transactions and submit the following:
Additional supporting documents to justify the difference between number of pax paid and the number of personnel ordered to attend the activity;
Menu for the breakfast, lunch and dinner was not attached; and
Attendance Sheet.

We recommended and the Management agreed to submit the following required supporting documents for the repair various equipment and motor vehicles totaling P157,108.78:
Pre-repair inspection reports;
Waste Material reports; and
Post-inspection reports.
We recommended and the management agreed to reconcile records regarding the forwarded balances and reflect adjustments in the books or remit the unremitted balances to the GSIS.

We recommended and the management agreed to cause the settlement of the ND Nos. 2024-001 to 006-101(2023) issued for the excess travelling expenses amounting to P10,800.00 and strictly comply with the provisions of EO No. 77 on subsequent travel claims.

Use the Other Professional Services account for the provision of honorarium to various national agency personnel assigned to the municipality and to properly attach supporting documents to the Disbursement Voucher.

Prepare and submit to COA office, the prescribed form on Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption prescribed under COA Circular 77-61 dated            September 26, 1977; and

Use the proper format and properly fill up the Driver’s Trip Ticket.
Submit the Monthly Report on Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to this office.

Continue the utilization of its 20% DF in accordance with the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1, dated November 4, 2020. Continue to strengthen the development planning and project prioritization of PPAs that would have a greater impact on the development of the Municipality that would redound to the benefit of the greater populace.

We recommended and the management agreed that:

The General Services Office and the Accounting Office reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of accounts; and
Management prepare and submit a duly accomplished RCPPE, complete with all the information as required.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LGU strictly enforce the posting of warranty security by the contractors after the issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance by the agency pursuant to the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
To ensure that the warranty security is posted, the LGU may make it a requirement before returning the 10 percent retention money to the contractor one year after the completion of the project after which the warranty period shall commence.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer:
Immediately deposit the remaining cash balance on hand as of   January 31, 2022 in the total amount of ₱1.468 million with the depository banks and furnish the Audit Team with a copy of the deposit slips; and
Deposit all collections daily and intact with the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Paragraph 1 of Section 69 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer Reconcile and account for the discrepancy of the Cash Local Treasury account between the accounting record and the cashbook amounting to ₱233,761.38 and thereafter record the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and
The Municipal Treasurer attach validated deposit slips in the RCDs.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
a.	Require the end user/in-charge of the program to sign the Job Order and Purchase Requests on future procurement of goods and services;
b.	Attach the menu of snacks and meals catered on the disbursement vouchers as well as pictures during the catered dates; and
c.	Submit the duly certified Attendance sheet/list of the recipient of the catered snacks, meals and bottled water as well as the distribution list and proof of receipt for the recipient of the goods for distribution to the affected household.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2022 and years thereafter the relevant disclosures on the LDRRMF required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the Financial Statements for a fairer presentation of accounts under the fund.
We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the SWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:


Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution which should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.

Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP).

Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.

Follow-up on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

We recommended that MRFs be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays. The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.

We recommended and the management agreed that:

The General Services Office and the Accounting Office reconcile the RPCPPE with the books of accounts; and
Management prepare and submit a duly accomplished RCPPE, complete with all the information as required.
observations.reason: The GSO submitted the RCPPE, however, it was made per Office and not per account title which makes it hard to reconcile. Moreover, they were unable to conduct a physical count, thus, the RCPPE submitted is not updated as to its completeness.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer deposit all collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was able to deposit the undeposited amount. However deposits of collections was not made intact and within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the unliquidated cash advance be liquidated and that the management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, COA Cir. No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and PD 1445.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly comply with the mandatory posting requirements of the updated APP, NOA, and other information relative to the Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act in the GPPB Online Portal as mandated in GPPB Circular No. 1-2020 to promote transparency and accountability for all emergency procurements relative in the implementation of Bayanihan Act.

We recommended and the management agreed that necessary action on the settlement of the Notices of Suspensions, Notices of Disallowance and Notices of Charges as of December 31, 2020 in the total amount of P65,000.00 will be done.

We recommended and the management agreed that necessary action on the settlement of the Notices of Suspensions, Notices of Disallowance and Notices of Charges as of December 31, 2020 in the total amount of P65,000.00 will be done.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the physical count and to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2016 and every year thereafter which should be reconciled with the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has conducted a Physical Inventory and has submitted an RPCPPE. However, reconciliation was not done, thereby reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) make a representation to the Local Legislative Body to allocate funds for SWM Programs as presented in ten year SWM Plan in order for the Municipality to attain its goal to have a proper and appropriate solid waste management practices.
We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management has settled suspensions amounting to a total of P78,287.20. Required documents for settlement of remaining balance were submitted June 24, 2020

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality formulate and pass their GAD Code to support the LGU’s efforts in promoting gender equality in the municipality.

We recommended that:

Management subject all the attributed projects to the HGDG test
Follow the attribution guide properly in determining the attributable amount to the GAD Fund
The GAD Focal Person coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the budget for the municipality’s mainstreamed projects.
We recommended that the management exert effort to have its Solid Waste Management Plan for 2015 – 2024 approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation of cash advances and not to allow additional cash advance(s) to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that management require Municipal Accountant to cancel stale checks, record the transaction as Accounts Payable and then restore the amount to Cash in Bank, LCCA for more accurate and reliable Cash Accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit in full the ₱1.313 million and thereafter deposit all  collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol 1.
observations.managementAction: The municipal treasurer was able to deposit the undeposited collection. However, deposit of succeeding collections were not intact and within the prescribed period. Hence, reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:


Require the Municipal Engineer implement the programmed projects and close monitoring be made to make sure that they are implemented within the planned timeframe.
Implement the PPAs based on the PPAs identified by the Municipal Development Council as prioritized PPAs to be funded by 20 percent DF.  MDC must specify PPAs to be released to barangays as financial assistance as the case maybe.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the release of the financial assistance from 20 percent Development Fund to barangays as “Due from other LGUs” account and upon liquidation of the disbursements incurred, the account “Due From LGUs” will be close to “Subsidy to Other LGUs”; and
Implement PPAs in the 20 percent DF which are in the nature of high-impact investment or capital expenditures as envisioned by the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM and stop the practice of using the 20 percent DF for MOOE.
Explanation is sought why the ₱43,954.84 disbursed fund for MOOE will not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person put more effort in the implementation of the PPAs identified in its GAD Plan and Budget in accordance to Sec. 36 of Republic Act No. 9710 and to make sure that at least 5 percent of the Municipality’s Budget is utilized for GAD related PPAs.
observations.managementAction: The management was able to utilize 34.34 percent of total GAD Budget for CY 2020. However, the management was still unable to fully utilize its GAD Budget. Hence, reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct the HGDG test at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) make a representation to the Local Legislative Body to allocate funds for SWM Programs as presented in ten year SWM Plan in order for the Municipality to attain its goal to have a proper and appropriate solid waste management practices.
observations.managementAction: Management will be updating their 10 year SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol 1.
We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation of cash advances and not to allow additional cash advance(s) to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that management require Municipal Accountant to cancel stale checks, record the transaction as Accounts Payable and then restore the amount to Cash in Bank, LCCA for more accurate and reliable Cash Accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit in full the ₱1.313 million and thereafter deposit all  collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol 1.

We recommended the following:
Require the Municipal Engineer implement the programmed projects and close monitoring be made to make sure that they are implemented within the planned timeframe.
Implement the PPAs based on the PPAs identified by the Municipal Development Council  as prioritized PPAs to be funded by 20 percent DF.  MDC must specify PPAs to be released to barangays as financial assistance as the case maybe.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record the release of the financial assistance from 20 percent Development Fund to barangays as “Due from other LGUs” account and upon liquidation of the disbursements incurred, the account “Due From LGUs” will be close to “Subsidy to Other LGUs”; and
Implement PPAs in the 20 percent DF which are in the nature of high-impact investment or capital expenditures as envisioned by the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DILG and DBM and stop the practice of using the 20 percent DF for MOOE.
Explanation is sought why the ₱43,954.84 disbursed fund for MOOE will not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person put more effort in the implementation of the PPAs identified in its GAD Plan and Budget in accordance to Sec. 36 of Republic Act No. 9710 and to make sure that at least 5% of the Municipality’s Budget is utilized for GAD related PPAs.
We recommended that management conduct the HGDG test at the end of project implementation and incorporate the results in determining the percentage of PPA expenditures attributable to GAD.

Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution which should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.

Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.
Follow-up on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

We recommended that MRFs be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays. The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit a GPB that is in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2016-01 and to maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit her collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol 1.
We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.

Follow the attribution guide properly in determining the attributable amount to the GAD Fund
The GAD Focal Person coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer in determining the budget for the municipality’s mainstreamed projects.

We recommended that the SWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:
Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution which should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.
Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP).
Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.
Follow-up on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

We recommended that MRFs be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays. The municipality may initially pilot a single barangay to establish MRF project in order to institute a system of knowledge in establishing one as well as to gain commitment/participation of barangay officials on the proper administration and the operation of MRF.

We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC), Local Code for Children (LCC), Local State of Children’s Report (LSCR) and Children’s Profile in compliance to Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circulars No. 2012-120 and to maximize the utilization of the LCPC Fund.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit exert efforts in locating the documents to support the request for the dropping of the particular accounts from the books and submit the schedule to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.  In case it is impossible to locate the same, a request should be made to transfer the same to the Registry of Dormant Account following the guidelines and procedures in dropping of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 2007.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the physical count and to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2016 and every year thereafter which should be reconciled with the books of accounts.

We recommended that the management stop using funds of LDRMMF to the program/activities that are not identified with the plans/program/activities presented as priority projects under the DRRM Plan.   On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant cause the proper adjustments of accounting entries of the expense accounts that were erroneously charged to the LDRRMF.  Also, the Municipal Accountant submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor on the prescribed date.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

1. intensify further awareness to the constituents thru creative information campaign and education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”, 

2. strictly enforce the sanctions to violators of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management, 

3. establish and develop an operational MRF for the biodegradable and recyclable wastes 

4. sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of a municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the above stated report within the deadline set as required by the above mentioned Circular.

We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC), Local Code for Children (LCC) and Children’s Profile in compliance to Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circulars No. 2012-120 and to maximize the utilization of the LCPC Fund.
We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the LDRRMO re-evaluate the LDRRM Utilization Program to ensure that programs and activities include only those that the municipality can realistically implement during the year and that the amounts appropriated for each program or activity is reasonable. Also, ensure that the LDRRM volunteers are Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers as required by Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: The management had started establishing GAD Database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 8 of COA Circular 93-382.
observations.managementAction: Details are discussed in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) make a presentation to the Local Legislative Body to allocate funds for SWM Programs as presented in 10 year SWM Plan in order for the Municipality to attain its goal to have a proper and appropriate solid waste management practices.
observations.managementAction: Details are discussed in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposits her collections daily and intact to the authorized depositories and stop the practice of retaining portion of the collections as cash reserve pursuant to Section 69(4) of P D. 1445 and Section 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol 1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 1. intensify further awareness to the constituents thru creative information campaign and education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”, 2. strictly enforce the sanctions to violators of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management, 3. establish and develop an operational MRF for the biodegradable and recyclable wastes 4. sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of a municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the above stated report within the deadline set as required by the above mentioned Circular.

We recommended that the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) make a representation to the Local Legislative Body to allocate funds for SWM Programs as presented in ten year SWM Plan in order for the Municipality to attain its goal to have a proper and appropriate solid waste management practices.

We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and Financial Plan, Local Development Plan for Children (LDPC), Local Investment Plan for Children (LIPC), Local Code for Children (LCC) and Children’s Profile in compliance to Section II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circulars No. 2012-120 and to maximize the utilization of the LCPC Fund.
We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit a GPB that is in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2016-01 and to maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund.
We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the LDRRMO reevaluate the LDRRM Utilization Program to ensure that programs and activities include only those that the municipality can realistically implement during the year and that the amounts appropriated for each program or activity is reasonable. Also, ensure that the LDRRM volunteers are Accredited Community Disaster Volunteers as required by Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Accounting Unit exert efforts in locating the documents to support the request for the dropping of the particular accounts from the books and submit the schedule to the Office of the Audit Team Leader.  In case it is impossible to locate the same, a request should be made to transfer the same to the Registry of Dormant Account following the guidelines and procedures in dropping of accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 2007.
observations.managementAction: Request to transfer of dormant accounts to the Registry of Dormant Accounts is on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials strictly adhere and comply with the basic rules in the utilization of fuel of government vehicles as provided for in Section V of COA Circular No.  75-6    November 7, 1975.

We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Board pass a resolution requiring every household to have a receptacle for the segregation of solid waste pending collections. Also, we recommend management provide appropriation for its Ecological Solid Waste Management, create a Solid Waste Management Board, develop the Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan; and adopt measures for and monitor its effective implementation.
observations.managementAction: Management is will be updating their 10 year SWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of unexpended /unobligated balance of QRF and DRRMF-MOOE as well as fund from other sources solely for MDRRM activities to Trust Fund.  The accrued amount shall be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk management activities within the next five years.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee complete the physical count and to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2016 and every year thereafter which should be reconciled with the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management has conducted a Physical Inventory and has submitted an RPCPPE. Reconciliation of the books is ongoing

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management cause the immediate submission of the employees’ individual accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employees’ contribution in compliance to the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2001.  The municipality’s PRAISE Committee must establish guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct of each employee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive intensify further awareness of the constituents thru creative information campaign and education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy” and strictly enforce the sanctions to violators of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management.

Moreover, establish and develop an operational MRF for the biodegradable and recyclable wastes and a sanitary landfill sites shall be developed and operated as a final disposal site for solid and, eventually, residual wastes of a municipality in compliance with the Solid Waste Management Act.
observations.managementAction: Details are discussed in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 36 of the Republic Act No. 9710 in order to recognize the role of women in nation building and ensure the fundamental gender equality.  Also, justify the disbursements not in line with GAD budget.

We recommended that the management stop using funds of LDRMMF to the program/activities that are not identified with the plans/program/activities presented as priority projects under the DRRM Plan.   On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant cause the proper adjustments of accounting entries of the expense accounts that were erroneously charged to the LDRRMF.  Also, the Municipal Accountant submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor on the prescribed date.
observations.managementAction: Details are discussed in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials strictly adhere and comply with the basic rules in the utilization of fuel of government vehicles as provided for in Section V of COA Circular No.  75-6    November 7, 1975.
observations.managementAction:
Trip tickets were submitted but not completely and correctly filled up.

From 2013 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Strictly observe daily recording of collections and deposits in the cashbooks;

Reconcile the cashbooks against the cash on hand on a daily basis for accuracy of balance at any given time; and

Deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily, or not later than the next banking day, to ensure protection from possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction:
As of December 31, 2016 cash in vault balance is ₱618,908.58.

Details are discussed in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund only for those identified priority programs and projects as enumerated in Section 3.0 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM dated April 13, 2011.  The utilization of the 20% Development Fund should partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures and cost recovery and entrepreneurship.

Submit an explanation/ clarification/justification for the utilization of the fund for purposes other than those enumerated in the aforementioned regulations.
observations.managementAction:
The municipality stopped charging unrelated expenses against the 20% DF.

From 2015 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local School Board should: (a) stop the charging of payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF; (b) ensure that SEF be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided for under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998; and (c) submit proof that extension classes were established, as recommended by the DECS Regional Director and approved by the DECS Secretary.
observations.managementAction:
Management has stopped charging payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes during the 2nd semester of 2016 to the SEF.

From 2015 AAR.
